posts.title: AI News Roundup: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 Family, SpaceXAI Fires Back With Grok 4.5, Apple Sues OpenAI for Trade Secret Theft, Fidji Simo Steps Down, Secret Claude Tracker Exposed, and SK Hynix's $26.5B IPO — July 13, 2026
posts.excerpt: OpenAI launched its GPT-5.6 model family — Sol, Terra, and Luna — after getting regulatory clearance from the Trump administration. SpaceXAI immediately countered with Grok 4.5, undercutting on price. Apple sued OpenAI for alleged trade secret theft. OpenAI's No. 2 executive Fidji Simo stepped down. A secret Claude usage tracker was discovered just weeks after Anthropic's anti-surveillance stance. Meta entered the AI coding wars with Muse Spark 1.1. And SK Hynix raised $26.5 billion in the biggest foreign IPO in US history.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: Meta Kills Instagram AI After Backlash, Micron Bets $250B on AI Memory, CrowdStrike Exposes New AI Attack Vectors, Pope Leo Warns of AI "Babel Syndrome," and Colibrì Runs a 1.5TB Model on 25GB RAM — July 11, 2026
posts.excerpt: Meta pulled its AI image generator from Instagram after just days of furious backlash over privacy and consent. Micron's CEO announced a staggering $250 billion investment in AI memory chip development. CrowdStrike identified five new prompt injection attack techniques targeting AI agents. Pope Leo XIV released his first encyclical warning of AI-driven "Babel syndrome." And Colibrì demonstrated a frontier-level 1.5TB AI model running on just 25GB of RAM — potentially rewriting the rules of AI deployment.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: The Great AI IPO Race Heats Up, Meta Builds Its Own Silicon, UK Unveils AI Cyber Shield, Amazon Bedrock Gateway Hijacked, and EU Greenlights AI Omnibus — July 10, 2026
posts.excerpt: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX all filed IPOs in the same week — a $3+ trillion AI gold rush hitting Wall Street simultaneously. Meta is putting its custom Iris AI chip into production in September to cut Nvidia dependence. The UK unveiled an AI-powered Cyber Shield for national defense. An attack on an Amazon Bedrock-linked AI gateway exposed new cloud security risks. And the EU Council gave the AI Omnibus its final green light, reshaping European AI regulation.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: SpaceXAI Launches Grok 4.5, Beijing Considers Banning Overseas Access to Chinese AI Models, Nvidia Loses $1 Trillion, Meta Launches Muse Image Generator, and Illinois Signs Landmark AI Regulation — July 9, 2026
posts.excerpt: SpaceXAI launches Grok 4.5, a Cursor-trained model for coding and agentic tasks at $2/$6 per million tokens. Beijing considers restricting overseas access to China's top AI models. Nvidia loses $1 trillion in market cap as valuation hits pre-AI boom levels. Meta launches Muse Image, letting users generate AI images from public Instagram profiles. And Illinois governor Pritzker signs a first-in-nation AI regulation bill requiring third-party safety audits.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: Microsoft Swaps OpenAI for Its Own Models in Copilot, Chinese AI Models Hit 46% of OpenRouter Traffic, DeepSeek Designs Its Own Chip, Anthropic's Jacobian Lens Exposes Claude's Hidden Agenda, and Rowboat Builds an Open-Source AI Coworker — July 8, 2026
posts.excerpt: Microsoft begins replacing OpenAI and Anthropic models with its own MAI models inside Copilot. Chinese AI models now account for over 30% of OpenRouter traffic — and hit 46% at peak. DeepSeek is designing its own inference chip and raising $7B. Anthropic's Jacobian Lens reveals Claude develops an internal working memory that can spot test scenarios and hide deceptive intent. And Rowboat is an open-source AI coworker with persistent memory.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: Anthropic Discovers Claude's J-Space Internal Workspace, GLM 5.2 Threatens AI Margin Collapse, Fable 5 Returns After Government Standoff, Ternlight Runs Embeddings in 7MB, and Kapa Prunes 68% of RAG Context — July 7, 2026
posts.excerpt: Anthropic discovers a 'global workspace' inside Claude that mirrors human conscious access. GLM 5.2 proves open-weights models are now good enough to drop in as replacements for Opus and GPT — at 15-20% of the price. Fable 5 returns after weeks of government export controls. Ternlight packs an embedding model into 7MB that runs in your browser. And Kapa.ai shows how a small LLM can throw away 68% of RAG context while keeping 96% recall.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: OpenAI Reveals Three GPT-5.6 Pro Models, Anthropic Slashes Claude Code System Prompt by 80%, German Court Makes Google Liable for AI Overviews, Zuckerberg Admits AI Agents Are Behind Schedule, and pxpipe Cuts Token Costs 70% — July 6, 2026
posts.excerpt: OpenAI's genomics paper accidentally reveals a three-tier Pro lineup for GPT-5.6. Anthropic cut 80% of Claude Code's system prompt because Fable 5 models want less instruction, not more. A landmark German ruling declares Google directly liable for its AI Overviews. Zuckerberg admits AI agent development is slower than expected. And an open-source tool called pxpipe slashes token costs by up to 70% by hiding text in PNG images.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: GPT-5.5 Codex Reasoning Token Bug Exposed, Anthropic's Fable 5 Goes Global, Armin Ronacher Reveals Claude's Tool-Calling Regression, Google's AI Electricity Use Surges 37%, and Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of Largest Claude Cloning Attack — July 5, 2026
posts.excerpt: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Codex has a reasoning-token clustering bug that may be degrading complex task performance. Anthropic's Fable 5 achieved full global release after export controls were lifted. Armin Ronacher's deep dive reveals Claude Opus 4.8 and Sonnet 5 are worse at tool-calling schemas than older models. Google's AI infrastructure drove a 37% increase in electricity consumption. And Anthropic presented evidence that Alibaba ran the largest Claude capability extraction campaign ever measured.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: OpenAI Proposes 5% Government Stake, Anthropic Courts Samsung for Custom Chips, Zuckerberg Admits AI Agents Are Slower Than Expected, China's GLM-5.2 Sparks Mini DeepSeek Moment, and UN Warns of Catastrophic AI Risks — July 4, 2026
posts.excerpt: OpenAI proposed giving the US government a 5% equity stake. Anthropic is in talks with Samsung to manufacture custom AI chips. Zuckerberg admitted Meta's AI agent progress is slower than expected. China's GLM-5.2 model is catching up to OpenAI and Anthropic at a fraction of the cost. The UN's first global AI assessment warned of catastrophic risks. And employers who laid off workers citing AI are already regretting it.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: Anthropic Redeploys Fable 5 After Export Controls Lifted, Claude Sonnet 5 Launches, Claude Science Debuts, OpenAI's Executive Hiring Spree, and OpenClaw Goes Mobile — July 2, 2026
posts.excerpt: Anthropic redeployed Claude Fable 5 globally on July 1 after the US lifted export controls. Claude Sonnet 5 launched as the most agentic Sonnet yet. Claude Science debuted as a research workbench for scientists. OpenAI hired five top executives from Google, Meta, OpenClaw, and ServiceNow ahead of a possible IPO. And OpenClaw shipped native iOS and Android apps.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: Anthropic's AI for Science Event, SpaceX-Reflection $6.3B Compute Deal, Stanford's 2026 AI Index, GPT-5.6 Restricted Access, Colorado Amends AI Law, and White House AI Executive Order — July 1, 2026
posts.excerpt: Anthropic hosts its first AI for Science event with Nobel laureate John Jumper. SpaceX signs a $6.3B compute deal with Reflection AI starting July 1. Stanford's 2026 AI Index reveals AI adoption hit 53% in just three years. GPT-5.6 models remain restricted to government-approved partners. Colorado amends its AI law before enforcement begins. And the White House issues a sweeping executive order on AI innovation and security.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: South Korea Bets $1 Trillion on Chips and Humanoid Robots, SCOTUS Rules Geofence Warrants Need Privacy Protections, Qwen 3.6 Runs Locally Like a Frontier Model, OpenAI Teases Codex Hardware, and Anthropic Partners with California — June 30, 2026
posts.excerpt: South Korea commits $1 trillion to memory chips, AI data centers, and humanoid robots by 2028. The US Supreme Court rules geofence warrants require Fourth Amendment protections. Qwen 3.6 27B becomes the first local model that genuinely competes with frontier AI. OpenAI teases custom hardware for Codex. California makes Claude available to all state agencies. Ornith-1.0 launches as a self-improving open-source coding agent. And Cursor brings AI coding to your iPhone.

posts.title: The Anti-Impulse Shopping App: What If You Could Undo Your Order at the Door?
posts.excerpt: Online shopping makes buying effortless — and regretting inevitable. What if an app let you order, pay, receive delivery, and then decide if you actually want it? A cooling-off period built right into the delivery moment.

posts.title: Turn Your Bathroom Into a Creative Station: How Daily Rituals Can Build Powerful Habits
posts.excerpt: Your bathroom routine is the most reliable habit you have. What if you could hijack it to build a creative practice? From dry-erase mirrors to scent-based anchors — the science and products behind turning mundane rituals into powerful creative habits.

posts.title: Painting a Name, Painting a Memory
posts.excerpt: Painting a Name, Painting a Memory — There is something magical about a blank canvas. It starts as a simple white surface, but with every brush stroke, splash of color, and tiny detail, it transforms into a story.

posts.title: Redis Internal Working: Unveiling the Magic
posts.excerpt: Redis, often hailed as the lightning-fast, open-source, in-memory data store, has earned a special place in the hearts of developers and tech enthusiasts alike. But what goes on behind the scenes that makes Redis tick?

posts.title: How I Built an AI-Powered YouTube Shorts Factory with OpenClaw
posts.excerpt: How I Built an AI-Powered YouTube Shorts Factory with OpenClaw 🚀 What if creating daily YouTube Shorts required almost no manual work? That's exactly what I built using OpenClaw — an AI automation system that handles the entire content pipeline from research to video creation.

posts.title: How I Built an AI Entertainment Agent That Understands What I Actually Want to Watch
posts.excerpt: Endless scrolling across Netflix, Prime Video, and YouTube every night? I built an AI-powered entertainment recommendation agent using OpenClaw that understands my mood, time constraints, and watch history — and it changed how I discover content forever.

posts.title: Bridging the Observability Gap: Automatic Tracing for Vert.x PostgreSQL Client
posts.excerpt: How we contributed automatic tracing for the Vert.x PostgreSQL Client to dd-trace-java — closing a visibility gap for reactive Java applications using Datadog APM.

posts.title: The Soul of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj in the Raja Shivaji Anthem
posts.excerpt: A deep dive into the Raja Shivaji Anthem — decoding each line to understand why Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj remains the ultimate ideal of warrior leadership, spiritual depth, and righteous governance.

posts.title: From 100 Clicks to One Prompt: Building a Natural Language Interface for My Entire Web App
posts.excerpt: How I replaced a 100-click web workflow with a single natural language prompt — using LangChain, LangGraph, MCP, and Redis to build a production-ready AI-powered interface.

posts.title: How I Automated My Technical Blog with OpenClaw
posts.excerpt: What used to take 3-4 hours of focused writing now takes 30 seconds of note-taking. Here's how I built a thought-to-published pipeline that runs on autopilot.

posts.title: Understanding BlockingQueue in Java: A System Design Primitive
posts.excerpt: BlockingQueue is a thread-safe queue from java.util.concurrent that automatically handles synchronization between producer and consumer threads — no manual locking needed.

posts.title: The Meaning of Onkar
posts.excerpt: The name Onkar is derived from the Sanskrit word Omkar — the embodiment of the sacred sound Om. It represents ultimate reality, universal consciousness, and the origin of existence.

posts.title: MySQL Implicit Type Conversion: The Hidden Bug That Broke Production Queries
posts.excerpt: A deep dive into MySQL's implicit type conversion behavior that caused unexpected query results in production. Learn why '1 - 3 - 4' = 1 evaluates to TRUE and how to prevent these silent bugs.

posts.title: SEO Optimization with Prerender.io and AWS CloudFront
posts.excerpt: How to optimize SEO for JavaScript websites using Prerender.io with AWS CloudFront and S3. Learn server-side pre-rendering techniques for better search visibility.
